frameworks.name: probabilistic inference
frameworks.one_line: Larvae combine noisy sensory signals via probabilistic computation

frameworks.name: reliability-weighted multisensory combination
frameworks.one_line: Cues weighted by reliability to reduce combined-signal variance

frameworks.name: data-driven agent-based model
frameworks.one_line: Behavioral agent fit to navigation tracks under varied noise

core_question: How does the Drosophila larva combine information across sensory modalities under varying reliability?
key_assumptions: Navigation behavior reflects underlying probabilistic inference
Optogenetically injected noise effectively manipulates signal reliability
Agent-based modeling can recover combination rules from tracks
manipulations: Configurations of odor and temperature gradients
Optogenetically-controlled sensory noise to vary signal reliability
key_findings: Larvae adapt navigation behavior to the reliability of individual sensory signals
In some configurations larvae minimize the variance of the combined signal
Exact combination mechanism appears to depend on the sensory modality involved
interpretations: Probabilistic inference directs natural orientation behaviors even in a small nervous system
Multisensory combination rules are not universal but modality-dependent
